capabilities.title: N2K 2018
capabilities.abstract: Land Cover/Land Use (LC/LU) status map as part of the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) Local Component, tailored to the needs of biodiversity monitoring in selected Natura2000 sites (4790 sites of natural and semi-natural grassland formations listed in Annex I of the Habitats Directive) including a 2km buffer zone surrounding the sites and covering an area of 631.820 km² across Europe. LC/LU is extracted from VHR satellite data and other available data. The reference year for the classification is 2018. For further information on the CLMS Local Component please refer to https://land.copernicus.eu/local.

layers.name: HRL_GRA:GRAC_C2018-2021
layers.title: CLMS_HRL_GrasslandChange_C2018-2021_R20m
layers.abstract: The HRL VLCC Grassland Change Layer 2018-2021 raster product has been created as part of the EEA Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS, https://land.copernicus.eu/). The HRL GRAC 1821 raster product at 20m resolution provides information on changes in grassland vegetation cover between the reference years 2018 and 2021. The thematic classes indicate all non-grassland areas, grassland gain and grassland loss, unchanged grassland in both years and unverified grassland gain and loss areas for the pan-European area of EEA38. For the change detection, it is specifically important to include a sophisticated time-series analysis into the herbaceous classification to prevent false class-flips between consecutive years and therefore to avoid artificial changes introduced by classification noise. This specific part is achieved by calculating the (fast) dynamic time warp (DTW) of the time-series of two subsequent years (Salvador et. al. 2007). For this analysis, the High Resolution Vegetation Phenology and Productivity– Plant Phenology Index (HR VPP PPI)  time-series. More about the High Resolution Layers and CLMS datasets can be found at https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products.

layers.name: HRL_GRA:GRA_S2017
layers.title: CLMS_HRL_Grassland_S2017_R10m
layers.abstract: The yearly HRL VLCC Grassland raster product has been created as part of the EEA Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS, https://land.copernicus.eu/). It provides a basic land cover classification with 2 thematic classes (grassland / non-grassland) at 10m spatial resolution and will cover the pan-European area of EEA38. The grassland areas have to be stable for a period of 6 years to be mapped in the Grassland Status Layer i.e. areas with detected ploughing events are excluded using the Ploughing Indicator (PLOUGH). Grass cover within the context of the products is understood as herbaceous vegetation with at least 30% ground cover and with at least 30% graminoid species such as Poaceae, Cyperaceae and Juncaceae. Additional non-woody plants such as lichens, mosses and ferns can be tolerated. More about the High Resolution Layers and CLMS datasets can be found at https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products.

layers.name: HRL_GRA:HER_S2017
layers.title: CLMS_HRL_HerbaceousCover_S2017_R10m
layers.abstract: The yearly HRL VLCC Herbaceous raster product has been created as part of the EEA Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS, https://land.copernicus.eu/). It provides a basic land cover classification with 2 thematic classes (temporal and permanent herbaceous / non-herbaceous) at 10m spatial resolution and will cover the pan-European area of EEA38. The production of the herbaceous layer is primarily based on the probability estimates obtained from the Base Vegetation Layer (BVL) which also serves to harmonize the different HRL VLCC products, including Grassland, Forest and Cropland. The BVL is created annually (2017-2021) and using probability estimates of various land cover types. More about the High Resolution Layers and CLMS datasets can be found at https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products.

layers.name: HRL_GRA:PLOUGH_S2017
layers.title: CLMS_HRL_PloughingIndicator_S2017_R10m
layers.abstract: The yearly HRL VLCC PLOUGH layer has been created as part of the EEA Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS, https://land.copernicus.eu/). The HRL VLCC PLOUGH Layer continues the 2015 and 2018 PLOUGH Layer following a rolling archive principle by adding current information and removing historic years. It indicates the number of years since the last indication of ploughing within the permanent grassland area. The PLOUGH layer is produced for the years 2017-2021. PLOUGH is derived by taking into account the series of binary HER layers, the BVL classifications and HR VPP PPI (Plant Phenology Index) quantiles. BVL classes 4 (crop) and 7 (overlaying layer between herbaceous and crop) indicate a ploughing event. Low HR VPP PPI quantiles indicate low vegetation at a certain time of the year. For years with missing information (2016 and earlier) the ploughing information from the historic PLOUGH product is considered which causes some issues. It provides 10m spatial resolution and covers the pan-European area of EEA38. More about the High Resolution Layers and CLMS datasets can be found at https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products.

It provides 10m spatial resolution and covers the pan-European area of EEA38. More about the High Resolution Layers and CLMS datasets can be found at https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products.

layers.name: HRL_GRA:GRAC_C2018-2021
layers.title: CLMS_HRL_GrasslandChange_C2018-2021_R20m
layers.abstract: The HRL VLCC Grassland Change Layer 2018-2021 raster product has been created as part of the EEA Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS, https://land.copernicus.eu/). The HRL GRAC 1821 raster product at 20m resolution provides information on changes in grassland vegetation cover between the reference years 2018 and 2021. The thematic classes indicate all non-grassland areas, grassland gain and grassland loss, unchanged grassland in both years and unverified grassland gain and loss areas for the pan-European area of EEA38. For the change detection, it is specifically important to include a sophisticated time-series analysis into the herbaceous classification to prevent false class-flips between consecutive years and therefore to avoid artificial changes introduced by classification noise. This specific part is achieved by calculating the (fast) dynamic time warp (DTW) of the time-series of two subsequent years (Salvador et. al. 2007). For this analysis, the High Resolution Vegetation Phenology and Productivity– Plant Phenology Index (HR VPP PPI)  time-series. More about the High Resolution Layers and CLMS datasets can be found at https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products.

layers.name: HRL_GRA:GRA_S2017
layers.title: CLMS_HRL_Grassland_S2017_R10m
layers.abstract: The yearly HRL VLCC Grassland raster product has been created as part of the EEA Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS, https://land.copernicus.eu/). It provides a basic land cover classification with 2 thematic classes (grassland / non-grassland) at 10m spatial resolution and will cover the pan-European area of EEA38. The grassland areas have to be stable for a period of 6 years to be mapped in the Grassland Status Layer i.e. areas with detected ploughing events are excluded using the Ploughing Indicator (PLOUGH). Grass cover within the context of the products is understood as herbaceous vegetation with at least 30% ground cover and with at least 30% graminoid species such as Poaceae, Cyperaceae and Juncaceae. Additional non-woody plants such as lichens, mosses and ferns can be tolerated. More about the High Resolution Layers and CLMS datasets can be found at https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products.

layers.name: HRL_GRA:HER_S2017
layers.title: CLMS_HRL_HerbaceousCover_S2017_R10m
layers.abstract: The yearly HRL VLCC Herbaceous raster product has been created as part of the EEA Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS, https://land.copernicus.eu/). It provides a basic land cover classification with 2 thematic classes (temporal and permanent herbaceous / non-herbaceous) at 10m spatial resolution and will cover the pan-European area of EEA38. The production of the herbaceous layer is primarily based on the probability estimates obtained from the Base Vegetation Layer (BVL) which also serves to harmonize the different HRL VLCC products, including Grassland, Forest and Cropland. The BVL is created annually (2017-2021) and using probability estimates of various land cover types. More about the High Resolution Layers and CLMS datasets can be found at https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products.

layers.name: HRL_GRA:PLOUGH_S2017
layers.title: CLMS_HRL_PloughingIndicator_S2017_R10m
layers.abstract: The yearly HRL VLCC PLOUGH layer has been created as part of the EEA Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS, https://land.copernicus.eu/). The HRL VLCC PLOUGH Layer continues the 2015 and 2018 PLOUGH Layer following a rolling archive principle by adding current information and removing historic years. It indicates the number of years since the last indication of ploughing within the permanent grassland area. The PLOUGH layer is produced for the years 2017-2021. PLOUGH is derived by taking into account the series of binary HER layers, the BVL classifications and HR VPP PPI (Plant Phenology Index) quantiles. BVL classes 4 (crop) and 7 (overlaying layer between herbaceous and crop) indicate a ploughing event. Low HR VPP PPI quantiles indicate low vegetation at a certain time of the year. For years with missing information (2016 and earlier) the ploughing information from the historic PLOUGH product is considered which causes some issues. layers.abstract: The Tree Cover Density (TCD) layer provides information on the proportional crown coverage per pixel at 10m spatial resolution and ranges from 0% (all non-tree covered areas) to 100%. The TCD is defined as the „vertical projection of tree crowns to a horizontal earth’s surface.
